claims.name: Probe peak Pearson r tb-2
claims.statement: A ridge probe on Gemma-3-27B residual-stream activations at the second-to-last turn-boundary token attains a peak held-out Pearson correlation of 0.835 with default-persona Thurstonian utilities at layer 29, across the 20 sampled layers.
claims.derivation: Read probe_metrics.json; find the layer maximising tb-2[L].pearson_r; report the maximum; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Probe peak layer tb-2
claims.statement: The tb:-2-selector ridge probe on Gemma-3-27B peaks at layer 29 among the 20 layers sampled every three blocks.

claims.name: Probe peak Pearson r eot
claims.statement: A ridge probe on Gemma-3-27B residual-stream activations at the end-of-turn token attains a peak held-out Pearson correlation of 0.825 with default-persona Thurstonian utilities at layer 32.
claims.derivation: Read probe_metrics.json; find the layer maximising eot[L].pearson_r; report the maximum; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Contrastive steering P a at L23 c neg 0.05 eot
claims.statement: Contrastive steering along the eot probe direction at layer 23 with $c = -5\%$ of the mean activation norm drives $P(\text{chose higher-utility task}) = 0.013$ on a 50-pair test sample.
claims.derivation: Filter rows to layer==23 and signed_multiplier==-0.05; P(choice_original=='a'); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Contrastive steering P a at L23 c pos 0.05 eot
claims.statement: Contrastive steering along the eot probe direction at layer 23 with $c = +5\%$ raises $P(\text{chose higher-utility task}) = 0.963$.
claims.derivation: Filter rows to layer==23 and signed_multiplier==+0.05; P(choice_original=='a'); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Contrastive preference swing L23
claims.statement: At layer 23 with $|c| = 5\%$, contrastive steering along the eot probe produces a 0.95-point swing in $P(\text{chose higher-utility task})$ from 0.013 at $c = -5\%$ to 0.963 at $c = +5\%$.
claims.derivation: |P(a)@+5% - P(a)@-5%| at layer 23 in eot_probe_L23.parsed.jsonl; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Contrastive preference swing L23 tb-2
claims.statement: Using the tb:-2 probe instead of eot at the same layer and coefficient yields a smaller but still substantial 0.49-point preference swing.
claims.derivation: |P(a)@+5% - P(a)@-5%| at layer 23 in tb-2_probe_L23.parsed.jsonl; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task first span baseline
claims.statement: Without any steering, the model picks the first-presented task at P = 0.56 (measured empirically from the flat layers L2-L14 and L35-L59 in the single-task sweep, where the dose-response is null).
claims.derivation: Restrict to condition=='unilateral_first' and layer in dead_layers [2,5,8,11,14,35,38,41,44,47,50,53,56,59]; compute the fraction of trials where the model picked whichever task was in first position (a at ordering=0, b at ordering=1); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task second span baseline
claims.statement: Without any steering, the model picks the second-presented task at P = 0.435 (same flat-layer measurement as the first-span baseline, so the two baselines sum to 1 up to refusals).
claims.derivation: Same as first-span baseline but condition=='unilateral_second' and target = task in second position.

claims.name: Single task position bias gap
claims.statement: The baseline position bias --- first-span minus second-span P(picked) with no steering --- is 0.12, indicating the model has a mild preference for the first-presented option independent of utility.

claims.name: Single task aggregate P steered at L23 c pos 0.05
claims.statement: At layer 23 with an applied coefficient of +5\% on a single task's tokens, the model picks that task at P = 0.623, averaged across which of the two physical tasks was steered.
claims.derivation: Aggregate over both unilateral_first and unilateral_second conditions at layer==23 where the applied coefficient (= signed_multiplier * (+1 if ordering==0 else -1)) equals +0.05; compute P(choice_original == physical task in steered span); round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task aggregate P steered at L23 c neg 0.05
claims.statement: At layer 23 with an applied coefficient of -5\% on a single task's tokens, the model picks that task at P = 0.142.
claims.derivation: Same as the +5% version but applied coefficient == -0.05.

claims.name: Single task aggregate swing L23
claims.statement: Single-task steering at layer 23 produces a 0.48-point swing in P(picked the steered task) between c = -5\% (0.142) and c = +5\% (0.623).
claims.derivation: aggregate(+0.05) - aggregate(-0.05) at layer 23; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task suppression L23
claims.statement: At layer 23, applying c = -5\% to a task's tokens drops P(pick that task) by 0.36 below the no-steering baseline (from 0.498 to 0.142).
claims.derivation: aggregate_baseline - aggregate(-0.05) at layer 23; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Single task amplification L23
claims.statement: At layer 23, applying c = +5\% to a task's tokens raises P(pick that task) by only 0.12 above the no-steering baseline (0.498 to 0.623) --- markedly less than the symmetric suppression delta.
claims.derivation: aggregate(+0.05) - aggregate_baseline at layer 23; round to 3dp.

claims.name: Steering max refusal rate percent
claims.statement: Across every (layer, coefficient, steering variant) bucket in the layer-sweep experiment --- 20 layers for both selectors, contrastive and single-task --- the worst-case refusal rate is 3.0\%. Steering does not operate near the coherence-collapse regime at these coefficients.
claims.derivation: For each (file, layer, signed_multiplier) bucket, compute fraction of rows with choice_original not in {'a','b'}; take max across all buckets; express as percent; round to 2dp.

claims.name: Contrastive steering working layer range lo
claims.statement: Contrastive steering along the eot probe direction produces a preference swing of at least 0.30 at every self-layer cell from layer 17 through layer 26.
claims.derivation: For each of the 20 sampled layers L, compute |P(a)@+5% - P(a)@-5%| at the eot probe's self-cell; keep Ls with swing >= 0.30; report min.

claims.name: Contrastive steering working layer range hi
claims.statement: The upper end of the working steering window is layer 26; at every layer L > 26 in the sweep, the self-layer contrastive swing drops below 0.30.
claims.derivation: Same as the lo claim but report max of the filtered layers.
